PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-21-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The matter was called with case number 289467 (Conservatorship of Marjorie Martin).

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for the Petitioner. Lucy Mejia for Clyde Martin (Objector).

The Court expressed concern with Mr. Martin using his authority to withdraw funds from the joint account. Ms. Rasch stated that this was done prior to the appointment of the conservatorship and that the joint tenancy was severed at that time. In addition, she stated that this should be raised in the estate so that all interested parties could have the opportunity object to the matter, that Mr. Martin waived entitlement as he waited 40 days to file his petition, and that there was concern about his health and ability to serve. Ms. Mejia stated that the mother allowed the withdrawal and his position was that the funds were his. Ms. Mejia argued that Ms. Rasch's petition was also late, that Mr. Martin's condition was stable, and that he deserved the opportunity to serve. In regards to the concern about undue influence from the care taker, Ms. Mejia stated that the care taker was no longer working with Mr. Martin and that she would keep a close eye on him. The Court stated that Mr. Martin had priority. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Bonner.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-21-2008 Text Ruling

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-05-2008 Continued To Mar-12-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Louise Martin-Carpenter and Lynn Perkins. Lucy Mejia for Clyde Martin (Petitioner).

Ms. Rasch informed the Court that the verificiation was filed this morning. Ms. Mejia requested a continuance stating that she objected to the objection being filed on Monday which she received by fax, and that she did not have a chance to submit a declaration. Ms. Rasch stated that the matter could be disposed based on statute as they waited 4 months to file their petition, and that there was concern raised as to the capacity to serve. Ms. Mejia stated that a Neuro/Psych eavaluation was to be done by the V.A. The matter was continued to March 12, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.
